ranked_movies.title: The Lobster
ranked_movies.reason: The most discussed film in the provided data. The original post sparked extensive conversation about its unique dystopian premise, dark humor, and emotional impact, leading to numerous recommendations for similar films and other works by Yorgos Lanthimos.

ranked_movies.title: The Act of Killing
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended multiple times as a mind-blowing and unsettling documentary that profoundly changes one's perspective. Described as a film that explores the aftermath of mass murder in a uniquely bizarre and impactful way.

ranked_movies.title: Three Identical Strangers
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently cited as a must-watch documentary that is shocking, eye-opening, and leaves a lasting impact on the viewer. Mentioned by multiple users across different posts.

ranked_movies.title: The Killing of a Sacred Deer
ranked_movies.reason: Strongly recommended as the next logical step for viewers of 'The Lobster'. Discussed for its ability to provoke intense emotional reactions (anger, fascination) and its connection to director Yorgos Lanthimos.

ranked_movies.title: Irreversible
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a genuinely good yet profoundly traumatizing film. Noted for its intentional use of sound design to create physical discomfort in the viewer and its impactful, unsettling narrative.

ranked_movies.title: Happiness
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a chilling 'comedy' with intersecting storylines that is both hilarious and horrifying. Cited as a film that will severely fuck you up with intention.

ranked_movies.title: Requiem for a Dream
ranked_movies.reason: The catalyst for the request for traumatizing films. Described as the 'worst movie experience' that caused panic attacks and insomnia, setting the standard for what the OP was seeking.

ranked_movies.title: Mysterious Skin
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a film that shakes viewers to their core with its extremely dark themes and powerful performances. Described as a genuinely good movie that fucks you up.

ranked_movies.title: The Jinx
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as a mind-blowing documentary series (often referred to as a film in the context) that is intense, eye-opening, and features a shocking real-life narrative.

ranked_movies.title: Kids
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for its raw, impactful portrayal of teenage life and HIV in NYC. Described as a film with an ending that is 'burned in your memory'.

ranked_movies.title: Logan
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed as a movie that exceeded all expectations, transforming from a typical superhero film into a brutal, character-driven masterpiece with compelling story and action.

ranked_movies.title: Dogtooth
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended alongside other Yorgos Lanthimos films. Described as a movie that viewers can't stop thinking about long after watching it.

ranked_movies.title: The Emperor's New Groove
ranked_movies.reason: Unexpectedly praised as one of Disney's best films. Discussed for its originality, humor, and how it earned its love despite a troubled production and lack of promotion.

ranked_movies.title: The Magnificent Ambersons
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of a major discussion about film preservation, studio interference, and the ethics of using AI to reconstruct lost footage. Its historical significance and tragedy were central to the conversation.

ranked_movies.title: Gladiator
ranked_movies.reason: Used as a benchmark for quality in the discussion about its sequel, 'Gladiator 2'. The original is held in high regard for its spectacle and story, which the sequel was criticized for lacking.

ranked_movies.title: Event Horizon
ranked_movies.reason: Cited for having one of the best comedic moments in a serious scene ('We're leaving'), which was highlighted as brilliantly funny and realistic within a horrifying context.

ranked_movies.title: Galaxy Quest
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a movie that far exceeded low expectations. Praised for its humor and originality, with specific callouts to its character performances.

ranked_movies.title: Boogie Nights
ranked_movies.reason: Referenced in the discussion about 70s pornographic films that were cinematic. Cited as a 'real film' that accurately portrayed the era of porn with plots, acting, and sets.

ranked_movies.title: The Thing
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted for a classic comedic moment in a serious scene ('I'd rather not spend the rest of this winter TIED TO THIS FUCKING COUCH!'), which was praised for its timing and realism under pressure.

ranked_movies.title: Wristcutters: A Love Story
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a film similar to 'The Lobster' in its level of quirky, dark humor. Described as a good, unconventional love story.
